evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Areas/AI Native Transformation.md already has user uncommitted changes; Inbox/AI Native Team Adoption Material.md clearly needs to update this Area supporting note. After organizing, the dirty supporting note should be protected.
evals.expected_output: The Inbox file is not moved or committed; Areas/AI Native Transformation.md stays uncommitted and unstaged; the log appends commit: none and notes the supporting note has uncommitted changes.
expectations: Record pre-organize non-Inbox dirty paths and treat Areas/AI Native Transformation.md as a protected path.
Inbox/AI Native Team Adoption Material.md is not moved and no organize commit is created.
The user's existing changes to Areas/AI Native Transformation.md are not staged or committed.
.claude/organize.log appends commit: none and notes the supporting note has uncommitted changes.

evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Inbox/photo.png is a screenshot. Organizing should first generate Inbox/photo.md, then follow the Markdown flow to classify, distill, add supporting notes, move, stage, commit, and log using the original screenshot content; if the screenshot is unreadable or lacks information, the original stays in Inbox with a clear reason.
evals.expected_output: photo.png produces photo.md via safe-markitdown; what gets organized is the Markdown supplemented with the screenshot's topic and key information, keeping the original image reference; do not submit only the screenshot placeholder template.
expectations: Run organize_inbox.py prepare first, and have the script call .claude/bin/safe-markitdown on Inbox/photo.png instead of leaving it as an unsupported type.
After conversion, Read the generated Inbox/photo.md and supplement topic, key information, and follow-up actions from the original screenshot.
The organized Markdown contains the organize marker, original image reference, necessary distillation, and 1-3 related wikilinks.
When the screenshot is unreadable, output is empty, content is unreadable, or information is insufficient, photo.png stays in Inbox and no empty-shell photo.md is committed.
.claude/organize.log records the move result or retention reason, and the commit hash comes only from the just-completed commit.

evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Inbox/report.pdf and Inbox/report.md have a same-name conflict, and it cannot be confirmed that report.md is the corresponding content note. After organizing, report.md must not be overwritten, and no self-generated dedup copy may be created.
evals.expected_output: Both report.pdf and report.md stay in Inbox; report.md's original content is unchanged; no organize commit is created; the log appends commit: none and notes the same-name Markdown conflict needs manual handling.
expectations: report.pdf stays in Inbox and report.md's original content is not overwritten.
No report-1.md or other dedup Markdown copy is generated.
No organize commit is created.
.claude/organize.log appends commit: none and notes the same-name Markdown conflict.

evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: there is an unrelated dirty file Areas/AI Native Transformation.md; Inbox/Standalone Tool Reference.md is Rust CLI reference material that does not need to update the dirty Area, but after entering Resources it should have a new Area supporting note.
evals.expected_output: Standalone Tool Reference is organized into Resources; a non-protected supporting Area is created or updated; the commit contains only the organized files and supporting note; the original dirty Area remains uncommitted and unstaged; the log hash is verifiable.
expectations: Inbox/Standalone Tool Reference.md is moved to a topic directory under Resources.
A non-protected Area/Project supporting note is created or updated and cross-linked with the resource note.
The organize commit does not include the existing dirty changes to Areas/AI Native Transformation.md.
The script exits successfully and Areas/AI Native Transformation.md is still an uncommitted change.
The commit hash in .claude/organize.log equals the latest commit and is git-verifiable.

evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Inbox/meeting.mp3 is a meeting audio note. Organizing should first transcribe it to Inbox/meeting.md, then follow the Markdown flow to classify, distill, add supporting notes, move, stage, commit, and log; if Whisper transcription fails, the audio stays in Inbox with a clear reason.
evals.expected_output: meeting.mp3 first produces meeting.md via safe-markitdown; on successful transcription what gets organized is meeting.md keeping the original audio reference, and the original audio may move with the note to the same target or Sources; on transcription failure the original audio is not moved and no empty-shell Markdown is committed, and the log states the transcription failure reason.
expectations: Call .claude/bin/safe-markitdown on Inbox/meeting.mp3 instead of moving the audio directly.
After transcription, Read the generated Inbox/meeting.md and treat that Markdown as the object to organize.
The organized Markdown contains the organize marker, original audio reference, necessary distillation, and 1-3 related wikilinks.
When transcription fails, output is empty, or content is unreadable, meeting.mp3 stays in Inbox and no empty-shell meeting.md is committed.
.claude/organize.log records the move result or retention reason, and the commit hash comes only from the just-completed commit.

The organized Markdown contains the organize marker, original CSV reference, necessary distillation, and 1-3 related wikilinks.
When conversion fails, output is empty, or content is unreadable, export.csv stays in Inbox and no empty-shell export.md is committed.
.claude/organize.log records the move result or retention reason, and the commit hash comes only from the just-completed commit.

evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Inbox/article.html is a web page export and Inbox/experiment.ipynb is an AI experiment Notebook. Both should first be converted to Markdown when organizing, then follow the Markdown flow to classify, distill, add supporting notes, move, stage, commit, and log.
evals.expected_output: Both article.html and experiment.ipynb produce same-name Markdown via safe-markitdown; on successful conversion organize the Markdown keeping the original file references; on conversion failure the original files stay in Inbox with a clear reason.
expectations: Call .claude/bin/safe-markitdown on Inbox/article.html and Inbox/experiment.ipynb instead of leaving them as unsupported types.
After conversion, Read the generated same-name Markdown and treat it as the object to organize.
The organized Markdown contains the organize marker, original file reference, necessary distillation, and 1-3 related wikilinks.
Pass the supporting-note gate before entering Resources or Archive, creating or updating Area/Project supporting notes when needed.
When conversion fails, output is empty, or content is unreadable, the original files stay in Inbox and no empty-shell Markdown is committed.

evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Inbox/whiteboard.jpeg is a whiteboard photo. Organizing should first generate Inbox/whiteboard.md, then follow the Markdown flow to classify, distill, add supporting notes, move, stage, commit, and log using the original screenshot content.
evals.expected_output: whiteboard.jpeg produces whiteboard.md via safe-markitdown; the organized Markdown is not just a placeholder template but includes distillation of the whiteboard photo's topic, key points, and next steps, keeping the original JPEG reference.
expectations: Call .claude/bin/safe-markitdown on Inbox/whiteboard.jpeg instead of leaving it as an unsupported type.
After conversion, Read the generated Inbox/whiteboard.md and supplement topic, key information, and follow-up actions from the original screenshot.
The organized Markdown contains the organize marker, original image reference, necessary distillation, and 1-3 related wikilinks.
Pass the supporting-note gate before entering Resources or Archive, creating or updating Area/Project supporting notes when needed.
When the screenshot is unreadable, output is empty, content is unreadable, or information is insufficient, the original file stays in Inbox and no empty-shell Markdown is committed.

evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Resources/Loop Engineering/Existing Article.md already has source_url and content_fingerprint; the next day Inbox/existing-copy.md appears with a source URL or body fingerprint identical to the existing note.
evals.expected_output: existing-copy.md is not organized into a new Resource; keep the existing canonical note, move the duplicate Inbox file to Archive/Duplicates/, and write canonical, duplication evidence, and organize date at the top of the duplicate file; the log records the duplicate handling.
expectations: Run organize_inbox.py prepare or apply-duplicates first, having the script extract Inbox/existing-copy.md's source URL and content_fingerprint and search Projects/Areas/Resources/Archive.
When the script matches an existing source_url, canonical_url, or content_fingerprint, it is judged an exact duplicate.
No new Resources material note is created and no unrelated supporting note is updated.
The duplicate file is moved via git mv to Archive/Duplicates/ or its topic subdirectory, and the file content states canonical and duplication evidence.
.claude/organize.log records the duplicate handling result, and the commit hash comes only from the just-completed commit.

evals.prompt: Organize Inbox in a temporary brain vault. Scenario: Inbox/loop-practice.md and the existing Resources/Loop Engineering/theory.md both belong to the Loop Engineering topic, with similar titles and keywords, but different source URLs and different body fingerprints.
evals.expected_output: loop-practice.md should not be treated as an exact duplicate; it should be organized into Resources as independent material, add possibly-related wikilinks, and update the corresponding Area/Project supporting note.
expectations: The script generates loop-practice.md's content_fingerprint before classification and compares it with existing notes.
Only topic/title similarity with different URL and fingerprint does not auto-archive as duplicate.
loop-practice.md enters Resources as independent material and writes source_url (if any) and content_fingerprint.
The organized content adds a possibly-related [[theory]] wikilink or a relation note.
Update the non-protected Area/Project supporting note per the supporting-note gate.
